label: Santiago
sub: Capital founded 1541; seat of government; La Moneda bombed September 11, 1973

label: La Moneda Palace
sub: Presidential palace bombed by air force jets on September 11, 1973; Allende died here

label: Villa Grimaldi
sub: DINA's primary torture and detention center (1974–1978); ~4,500 detained, 226 disappeared; now a Peace Park

label: Estadio Nacional
sub: Converted to detention and torture camp post-coup (Sept 1973); 7,000+ prisoners; Víctor Jara executed

label: Valparaíso
sub: Main Pacific port; seat of Congress since 1990; 2014 fire destroyed 3,000 homes

label: Iquique
sub: Battle of Iquique (May 21, 1879); Escuela Santa María massacre (Dec 21, 1907); nitrate port city

label: Chuquicamata
sub: World's largest open-pit copper mine; nationalized 1971; CODELCO's flagship asset

label: Antofagasta
sub: Formerly Bolivian port; taken by Chile in War of the Pacific (1879); copper and lithium export hub

label: Battle of Chacabuco
sub: Decisive patriot victory over royalists (February 12, 1817); secured Chile's independence

label: Temuco
sub: Capital of Araucanía region; center of Mapuche political and territorial struggle

label: Araucanía / Wallmapu
sub: Mapuche ancestral territory; site of ongoing land conflict and radical Mapuche resistance actions

label: Arica
sub: Northernmost Chilean city; taken from Peru in War of the Pacific (1880); administered under Lima peace protocol

label: Atacama Nitrate Fields
sub: Once world's primary source of saltpeter; drove War of the Pacific; replaced by copper post-WWI

label: Concepción
sub: Industrial heartland; second-largest city; major epicenter of 2019 estallido protests

label: Pisagua Detention Camp
sub: Post-coup detention camp in remote Atacama; mass graves discovered in 1990 during transition

label: Biobío River
sub: Historic boundary between Spanish colonial Chile and autonomous Mapuche territory (1641–1881)

label: Punta Arenas
sub: Southernmost large city; strategic Strait of Magellan control; birthplace of President Boric

label: Salar de Atacama
sub: World's largest lithium reserve; SQM and Albemarle operations; Boric national lithium strategy (2023)

label: El Teniente Mine
sub: World's largest underground copper mine; nationalized 1971; key CODELCO asset near Rancagua

label: Curalaba
sub: Battle of Curalaba (1598): Mapuche forces under Pelantaro killed Governor Óñez de Loyola, halting Spanish southward expansion
